budget_wines.name: Castello di Volpaia Chianti Classico DOCG
budget_wines.where_to_buy: Direct at Castello di Volpaia, Volpaia village, Radda in Chianti; also distributed widely in Italy and the United States.
budget_wines.description: High-elevation Radda annata from the medieval Volpaia village. Cool-site Sangiovese gives mountain-Chianti lift and red-cherry-and-violet poise for under twenty euros at the cellar door.
budget_wines.tip: The 18-euro at-cellar price for the latest annata is one of the best deals in Chianti Classico; order a case before leaving the village.

budget_wines.name: Felsina Berardenga Chianti Classico DOCG
budget_wines.where_to_buy: Felsina cellar door at Castelnuovo Berardenga; widely distributed in Italian wine shops and US importers (Domaine Select).
budget_wines.description: Felsina's entry-level Berardenga from the warm southern edge of Chianti Classico. Pure Sangiovese, dense plum-and-leather profile, the most reliable benchmark annata in the appellation.
budget_wines.tip: Always stable in style across vintages; great choice when shopping a Tuscan wine shop without scoring guidance.

budget_wines.name: Fontodi Chianti Classico DOCG
budget_wines.where_to_buy: Fontodi cellar at Panzano in Chianti; via Italian wine retailers and US importers.
budget_wines.description: Fontodi entry-level annata from Conca d'Oro in Panzano, certified organic since 2008. 100 percent Sangiovese, concentrated dark cherry and mineral, and one of the most consistently over-delivering values in the appellation.
budget_wines.tip: Best value Conca d'Oro Sangiovese in the under-25 band; cellar five years to taste it at its best.

budget_wines.name: Selvapiana Chianti Rufina DOCG
budget_wines.where_to_buy: Selvapiana cellar at Pontassieve; widely available in Italian wine retailers.
budget_wines.description: Certified-organic Sangiovese from the cool Sieve valley east of Florence. Pencil-shaving aromatics and a high-acid spine give Rufina its longevity reputation, all for under twenty euros.
budget_wines.tip: Rufina ages longer than most Chianti annate; lay one down for three to five years for a softer mid-palate.

budget_wines.name: Panizzi Vernaccia di San Gimignano DOCG
budget_wines.where_to_buy: Panizzi cellar in Santa Margherita, San Gimignano; broad Italian wine-shop distribution.
budget_wines.description: The Panizzi reference Vernaccia bottling pours bitter-almond, citrus pith and saline notes. The benchmark producer of Tuscany only DOCG white, often under fifteen euros retail.
budget_wines.tip: Drink it young and chilled with fritto misto di mare; the Riserva (around 20 euros) rewards two to three years of cellar.

budget_wines.name: Le Pupille Morellino di Scansano DOCG
budget_wines.where_to_buy: Le Pupille cellar at Istia d'Ombrone, Maremma; widely available in Italian retail and US import.
budget_wines.description: Elisabetta Geppetti reference Morellino. Sangiovese-led but generous and warm in the Maremma idiom: dark plum, Mediterranean herb and soft tannin for under twenty euros.
budget_wines.tip: Earlier-drinking than Chianti Classico annata; serve with grilled cinta senese pork or wild boar pappardelle.

budget_wines.name: Moris Farms Morellino di Scansano DOCG
budget_wines.where_to_buy: Moris Farms cellar at Massa Marittima; Italian wine retailers and superiore.de.
budget_wines.description: Parentini family Morellino, with a Sangiovese-led blend that captures the coastal Mediterranean Maremma character. Forward fruit, soft tannin, sub-twenty-euro price band.
budget_wines.tip: A textbook Maremma Sangiovese for dinner with rosemary lamb or pappa al pomodoro.

budget_wines.name: Castello Banfi Centine Toscana IGT
budget_wines.where_to_buy: Castello Banfi enoteca at Sant'Angelo Scalo, Montalcino; broad Italian retail and US Banfi distribution.
budget_wines.description: Castello Banfi entry-level IGT, named after a 19th-century farmhouse on the Montalcino estate. Sangiovese-Cabernet-Merlot blend made for everyday drinking at around twelve to thirteen euros.
budget_wines.tip: True under-15-euro Tuscan wine that punches above its weight on weeknight pasta with red sauce.

budget_wines.name: Rocca delle Macie Famiglia Zingarelli Chianti Classico DOCG
budget_wines.where_to_buy: Rocca delle Macie cellar at Castellina in Chianti; broad Italian retail distribution.
budget_wines.description: Castellina-based Zingarelli family blend pouring under twenty euros at most Italian retailers. A reliable Chianti Classico annata template: Sangiovese fruit, light international wood polish, ready to drink.
budget_wines.tip: Available in most Tuscan supermarkets; pair with cinghiale-sauce pappardelle on weeknights.

budget_wines.name: Cesani Vernaccia di San Gimignano DOCG
budget_wines.where_to_buy: Cesani cellar at Pancole, San Gimignano; small but reliable Italian distribution.
budget_wines.description: Family-run Cesani estate has bottled Vernaccia since 1980. Saline, lemon-zest annata that pours at the cellar for around twelve euros, a textbook Tuscan white at budget.
budget_wines.tip: Cellar visits at Pancole pair the Vernaccia with the estate's organic olive oil and chickpea pasta.
